content.thinking: The user just said "hey". This is a greeting. I should respond helpfully and ask what they'd like help with.

content.text:
Hey! 👋 I'm Claude Code, a coding assistant inside **pi**.

I can help you with all sorts of coding tasks:
- Reading and editing files
- Running commands
- Writing new code
- Debugging issues
- Working with frameworks and tools

What are you working on today?

{
  "name": "alvan-world-final",
  "version": "1.2.1",
  "description": "Building a best open source full stack ai webapp for opensource community",
  "author": "Alvan",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "concurrently \"next dev\" \"npm run sandbox:python\"",
    "dev:next": "next dev",
    "dev:webpack": "next dev --webpack",
    "dev:sandboxes": "concurrently \"npm run sandbox:python\"",
    "sandbox:python": "cd sandboxes && python python-sandbox/runner.py",
    "prebuild": "node scripts/setup-database.js --skip-sample",
    "build": "next build",
    "postbuild": "node -e \"console.log('Build complete. Run: npm run deploy')\"",
    "start": "next start",
    "start:production": "concurrently \"next start -p 3200\" \"npm run sandbox:python\"",
    "lint": "eslint",
    "lint:check": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx,.js,.jsx",
    "lint:fix": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx,.js,.jsx --fix",
    "lint:api": "eslint app/api --ext .ts --fix",
    "lint:components": "eslint components --ext .tsx --fix",
    "lint:lib": "eslint lib --ext .ts --fix",
    "type:check": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:ui": "vitest run --environment jsdom",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
    "setup:database": "node scripts/setup-database.js",
    "setup:db": "node scripts/setup-database.js",
    "setup:db:backup": "node scripts/setup-database.js --backup",
    "setup:db:force": "node scripts/setup-database.js --force --backup",
    "setup:db:clean": "node scripts/setup-database.js --skip-sample",
    "db:verify": "node scripts/setup-database.js --skip-sample --verbose",
    "db:check": "node -e \"require('./lib/database/production-check.ts').printProductionReport()\"",
    "db:production-ready": "NODE_ENV=production node -e \"require('./lib/database/production-check.ts').printProductionReport()\"",
    "admin:create": "node scripts/create-admin.js",
    "admin:setup": "node scripts/create-admin.js",
    "sync:prompt": "node scripts/sync-system-prompt.mjs",
    "sync:system-prompt": "node scripts/sync-system-prompt.mjs",
    "rebuild": "npm rebuild better-sqlite3",
    "setup:fresh": "npm rebuild better-sqlite3 && node scripts/setup-database.js",
    "setup:production": "NODE_ENV=production run setup:database && run admin:create",
    "migrate:messages": "node scripts/migrate-chat-messages.js",
    "db:migrate": "node scripts/setup-database.js && node scripts/migrate-chat-messages.js",
    "bench:stream": "bun scripts/bench-stream-parser.ts",
    "deploy": "node scripts/deploy.js",
    "wipe": "node scripts/wipe-server.js",
    "deploy:fresh": "node scripts/deploy.js --fresh"
  },

# 🌌 ALVAN WORLD > **Next-Generation AI Platform** - Where imagination meets intelligent visuals [![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-1.2.1-blue.svg)](https://github.com/yourusername/alvanworld) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green.svg)](LICENSE) [![Next.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-16.0-black.svg)](https://nextjs.org/) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.9-blue.svg)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) ALVAN World is a comprehensive, open-source AI platform built for the community. It provides a seamless interface for AI conversations, image generation, and intelligent collaboration, supporting multiple AI providers and models. ## ✨ Key Features ### 🤖 Multi-Provider AI Support - **Google Gemini** (2.5 Flash, 2.5 Pro, Image Generation) - **OpenAI** (GPT models via OpenAI-compatible API) - **Anthropic** (Claude models) - **Local Models** (LM Studio integration) - **Custom Providers** (OpenAI-compatible endpoints) ### 💬 Advanced Chat Features - Real-time streaming responses with typing indicators - Conversation history and search - Folder organization for chats - Pin important conversations - Share chats via unique links - Automatic title generation - Message reactions and interactions ### 🎨 Image Generation & Editing - Google Imagen 4.0 (Fast & Premium modes) - Image editing with natural language - Image gallery with filtering and search - Download and share generated images - Multi-image generation support ### 🛠️ AI Function Calling (Tools) - **Query Chat History** - Search through past conversations - **Summarize Text** - Generate concise summaries - **Action Plan Generator** - Create structured plans - **Meeting Agenda Builder** - Organize meetings efficiently - **QR Code Generator** - Create QR codes instantly - Extensible tool system for custom functions ### 👥 User Management - Role-based access control (Admin/User) - User API key management (BYOK - Bring Your Own Key) - Session management with JWT authentication - Profile customization with avatar upload - Activity tracking and audit logs ### 🔐 Security & Privacy - JWT-based authentication with secure sessions - Password hashing with bcrypt - API key encryption and secure storage - Configurable security settings (timeout, login attempts) - Audit logging for admin actions - CORS protection and rate limiting ### 📊 Admin Dashboard - Real-time analytics and statistics - User management and permissions - Model configuration and access control - System health monitoring - Audit log viewer - Security settings management ## 🏗️ Architecture ### Tech Stack **Frontend** - **Framework:** Next.js 16.0 (App Router) - **UI Library:** React 19.2 - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4.1 - **Component Library:** shadcn/ui + Radix UI - **Animations:** Framer Motion, GSAP - **State Management:** Zustand - **3D Graphics:** Three.js, React Three Fiber, Spline **Backend** - **Runtime:** Node.js with Next.js API Routes - **Database:** SQLite with better-sqlite3 (18 tables) - **AI SDKs:** Vercel AI SDK (@ai-sdk/*) - **Authentication:** JWT with jsonwebtoken - **Storage:** AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage, Local storage **AI Providers** - Google GenAI SDK (@google/genai) - OpenAI SDK (@ai-sdk/openai) - Anthropic SDK (@ai-sdk/anthropic) - LM Studio SDK (@lmstudio/sdk) - OpenAI-compatible providers ### Database Schema The application uses SQLite with 18 core tables: **User & Authentication** - `auth` - Authentication credentials - `user` - User profiles and settings - `user_sessions` - Active sessions with JWT tokens **Chat System** - `chat` - Conversation threads - `folder` - Chat organization **AI Models** - `model` - AI model configurations - `model_permission` - User access to models - `user_api_keys` - User-provided API keys (BYOK) **Content Management** - `file` - Uploaded files - `knowledge` - Knowledge base entries - `memory` - User memory for context **Collaboration** - `group` - User groups **Messaging System** - `channel` - Communication channels - `message` - Individual messages (referenced above under Chat System context) - `message_reaction` - Message reactions **System** - `tool` - Custom AI tools/functions - `function` - Reusable functions - `prompt` - Saved prompts - `tag` - Content tagging - `feedback` - User feedback - `system_settings` - Application configuration - `audit_logs` - Admin action logs ## 🚀 Getting Started ### Prerequisites - Node.js 18+ (recommended: 20+) - npm, pnpm, or yarn - Git ### Installation 1. **Clone the repository** ```bash git clone https://github.com/yourusername/alvanworld.git cd alvanworld ``` 2. **Install dependencies** ```bash pnpm install # or npm install # or yarn install ``` 3. **Set up environment variables** Create a `.env.local` file in the root directory: ```env # Database DATABASE_URL=./database/alvanworld.db # JWT Authentication JWT_SECRET=your-super-secret-jwt-key-change-this-in-production JWT_EXPIRES_IN=7d JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_IN=30d # AI Provider API Keys (Optional - Users can add their own) GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-google-api-key GEMINI_API_KEY=your-gemini-api-key OPENAI_API_KEY=your-openai-api-key ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-anthropic-api-key # Image Storage (Choose one) # Option 1: AWS S3 AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-aws-access-key AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-aws-secret AWS_REGION=us-east-1 AWS_S3_BUCKET=your-bucket-name # Option 2: Google Cloud Storage GCS_PROJECT_ID=your-project-id GCS_BUCKET_NAME=your-bucket-name GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=./path-to-credentials.json # Option 3: Local Storage (Default) LOCAL_UPLOAD_DIR=./uploads # Application Settings NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000 NODE_ENV=development ``` 4. **Initialize the database** ```bash pnpm run setup:database # or npm run setup:database ``` This will: - Create the SQLite database - Set up all 18 tables with optimized indexes - Enable WAL mode for better performance - Insert sample data (optional) 5. **Create an admin user** ```bash pnpm run admin:create # or npm run admin:create ``` Follow the prompts to create your admin account. 6. **Start the development server** ```bash pnpm dev # or npm run dev ``` Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) in your browser. ## 📖 Usage Guide ### For Users 1. **Sign Up/Login** - Navigate to `/auth` to create an account or login - Verify your email (if email verification is enabled) 2. **Start Chatting** - Click "New Chat" to start a conversation - Select an AI model from the dropdown - Type your message and press Enter or click Send - Upload images for vision-enabled models - Attach code files for analysis 3. **Generate Images** - Use the image generation tool in the prompt box - Enter a detailed description - Choose between Fast or Premium quality - Download or share generated images from the gallery 4. **Organize Chats** - Create folders to organize conversations - Pin important chats - Use global search to find past conversations - Archive completed chats 5. **Manage Your API Keys** - Go to Profile → API Keys - Add your own API keys for different providers - Use your own models (BYOK) - Toggle models on/off as needed ### For Admins 1. **Access Admin Dashboard** - Navigate to `/admin` - View system statistics and analytics 2. **User Management** - Create, edit, or delete users - Assign roles (Admin/User) - View user chat history - Reset user passwords 3. **Model Configuration** - Add new AI models - Configure model parameters - Set access permissions per user - Enable/disable models globally 4. **System Settings** - Configure security settings - Set JWT expiration times - Adjust session timeouts - Configure password requirements 5. **Monitor Activity** - View audit logs - Track user sessions - Monitor system health - Review error logs ## 🔧 Configuration ### Database Setup Options ```bash # Fresh setup with sample data pnpm run setup:database # Clean setup without sample data pnpm run setup:db:clean # Force recreation with backup pnpm run setup:db:force # Verify database integrity pnpm run db:verify # Check production readiness pnpm run db:production-ready ``` ### Environment Variables See `.env.example` for a complete list of configuration options. Key configurations: - Database path and settings - JWT secrets and expiration - AI provider API keys - Storage backend (S3/GCS/Local) - CORS and security settings - Feature flags ## 🏃 Development ### Available Scripts ```bash # Development pnpm dev # Start dev server pnpm build # Build for production pnpm start # Start production server # Code Quality pnpm lint # Run ESLint pnpm lint:fix # Fix linting issues pnpm type:check # Run TypeScript checks # Database pnpm setup:database # Initialize database pnpm admin:create # Create admin user pnpm db:verify # Verify database # Maintenance pnpm rebuild # Rebuild native dependencies ``` ### Project Structure ``` alvanworld/ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ ├── api/ # API Routes │ │ ├── admin/ # Admin endpoints │ │ ├── auth/ # Authentication │ │ ├── chat/ # Chat functionality │ │ ├── images/ # Image generation │ │ └── user/ # User management │ ├── admin/ # Admin pages │ ├── auth/ # Auth pages │ ├── chat/ # Chat interface │ └── gallery/ # Image gallery ├── components/ # React components │ ├── admin/ # Admin components │ ├── chat/ # Chat components │ ├── ui/ # UI primitives │ └── user/ # User components ├── lib/ # Core libraries │ ├── controllers/ # Business logic │ ├── repositories/ # Database access │ ├── services/ # External services │ │ ├── ai-provider-service.ts │ │ ├── auth-service.ts │ │ └── tools/ # AI function calling │ ├── middleware/ # Auth & routing │ └── types/ # TypeScript types ├── database/ # SQLite database ├── scripts/ # Utility scripts │ ├── setup-database.js # DB initialization │ └── create-admin.js # Admin creation └── public/ # Static assets ``` ## 🔌 API Documentation ### Authentication **POST** `/api/auth/register` - Create new user account - Body: `{ email, name, password }` **POST** `/api/auth/login` - Authenticate user - Body: `{ email, password }` - Returns: `{ user, token }` **POST** `/api/auth/logout` - Invalidate session - Headers: `Authorization: Bearer ` ### Chat **GET** `/api/chat` - List user's chats - Headers: `Authorization: Bearer ` **POST** `/api/chat` - Create new chat - Body: `{ title, folder_id? }` **POST** `/api/chat/[id]/stream` - Stream AI response - Body: `{ content, model, image?, thinking_enabled? }` ### Images **POST** `/api/images/generate` - Generate images - Body: `{ prompt, model, chatId? }` **GET** `/api/user/images` - List user's images ### Admin (Requires admin role) **GET** `/api/admin/users` - List all users **POST** `/api/admin/models` - Create AI model configuration **GET** `/api/admin/analytics` - Get system analytics See [API.md](docs/API.md) for complete API documentation (coming soon). ## 🎨 Customization ### Adding Custom AI Models 1. Navigate to Admin → Models 2. Click "Add Model" 3. Configure: - Provider (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Custom) - Model ID - Parameters (temperature, max_tokens, etc.) - Access permissions ### Creating Custom Tools Tools are AI functions that can be called during conversations. 1. Create a new tool in `lib/services/tools/`: ```typescript import type { Tool } from './types'; export const myCustomTool: Tool = { name: 'my_custom_tool', description: 'Does something useful', parameters: { type: 'object', properties: { input: { type: 'string', description: 'Input parameter' } }, required: ['input'] }, execute: async (args) => { // Tool implementation return { result: 'success' }; } }; ``` 2. Register in `lib/services/tools/index.ts`: ```typescript import { myCustomTool } from './my-custom-tool'; export function initializeTools() { toolRegistry.register(myCustomTool); // ... other tools } ``` ### Theming Customize the appearance in `app/globals.css`: ```css @layer base { :root { --background: 0 0% 100%; --foreground: 222.2 84% 4.9%; /* ... other variables */ } } ``` ## 🐳 Deployment ### Docker (Coming Soon) ```bash # Build image docker build -t alvanworld . # Run container docker run -p 3000:3000 \ -e JWT_SECRET=your-secret \ -e GOOGLE_API_KEY=your-key \ -v ./database:/app/database \ alvanworld ``` ### Vercel 1. Connect your GitHub repository 2. Configure environment variables 3. Deploy Note: SQLite requires persistent storage. Consider using PostgreSQL or MongoDB for serverless deployments. ### Traditional Hosting ```bash # Build application pnpm build # Set production environment export NODE_ENV=production # Start server pnpm start ``` Use PM2 for process management: ```bash pm2 start npm --name "alvanworld" -- start ``` ## 🧪 Testing ```bash # Run tests (when implemented) pnpm test # Type checking pnpm type:check # Linting pnpm lint ``` ## 🔧 Troubleshooting ### Common Issues **Database locked error** ```bash # SQLite database is locked # Solution: Close other connections, restart the app pnpm run setup:db:force # Creates backup and recreates DB ``` **better-sqlite3 compilation errors (Windows)** ```bash # Rebuild native dependencies pnpm rebuild better-sqlite3 # or npm rebuild better-sqlite3 ``` **Port 3000 already in use** ```bash # Kill the process using port 3000 # Windows: netstat -ano | findstr :3000 taskkill /PID /F # Mac/Linux: lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9 ``` **API Key not working** - Verify the API key is correct in `.env.local` - Ensure you've restarted the dev server after adding keys - Check the AI provider's dashboard for key validity - For user API keys, verify they're marked as active **Images not displaying** - Check storage configuration (AWS S3, GCS, or Local) - Verify storage credentials are correct - Ensure the storage bucket/directory has proper permissions - For local storage, check `LOCAL_UPLOAD_DIR` exists **Streaming responses not working** - Clear browser cache and cookies - Check browser console for errors - Verify the selected model supports streaming - Ensure your API key has sufficient credits/quota ## 🤝 Contributing We welcome contributions! Please see [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines (coming soon). 1. Fork the repository 2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`) 3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add amazing feature'`) 4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/amazing-feature`) 5. Open a Pull Request ## 📝 License This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details. ## 🙏 Acknowledgments - [Vercel AI SDK](https://sdk.vercel.ai/) - AI provider abstraction - [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/) - UI components - [Radix UI](https://www.radix-ui.com/) - Accessible primitives - [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/) - Animations - [Better SQLite3](https://github.com/WiseLibs/better-sqlite3) - Fast SQLite ## 📞 Support - 📧 Email: support@alvanworld.com - 💬 Discord: [Join our community](https://discord.gg/alvanworld) - 🐛 Issues: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/yourusername/alvanworld/issues) - 📖 Docs: [Documentation](https://docs.alvanworld.com) ## 🗺️ Roadmap - [ ] Multi-language support (i18n) - [ ] Voice input/output - [ ] Mobile apps (iOS/Android) - [ ] Plugin system for extensions - [ ] Advanced analytics dashboard - [ ] Team collaboration features - [ ] Vector database integration for RAG - [ ] Custom model fine-tuning - [ ] WebSocket support for real-time collaboration - [ ] Export conversations (PDF, Markdown) ---
Built with ❤️ by the ALVAN World team
Making AI accessible to everyone

content.text:
## 🔍 Project Overview: ALVAN WORLD

**A comprehensive, open-source AI platform** — version **1.2.1**

### 📊 Project Status
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| **Runtime** | Bun 1.3.11 + Node v22.14.0 |
| **Framework** | Next.js 16.2 + React 19.2 + TypeScript 5.9 |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 4.1 + shadcn/ui |
| **Database** | SQLite (better-sqlite3) with 18 tables |
| **Git Status** | Clean working tree (1 unstaged file: `next-env.d.ts`) |
| **Latest Commit** | `dfd9ccc` — landing & services updates |

---

### 🏗️ Architecture Overview

```
app/
├── api/          # API routes (admin, auth, chat, images, user)
├── admin/        # Admin dashboard
├── auth/         # Authentication pages
├── chat/         # Chat interface
├── gallery/      # Image gallery
├── dashboard/    # User dashboard
├── modern/       # Modern UI components
├── new/          # New features
└── globals.css   # Tailwind v4 theming

lib/
├── controllers/   # Business logic
├── repositories/# Data access
├── services/     # External services (AI providers, tools)
└── types/        # TypeScript definitions

components/      # React components
database/        # SQLite DB files
scripts/         # Setup & utility scripts
sandboxes/       # Python sandbox runner
```

---

### 🔑 Key Features
- **Multi-Provider AI**: Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, LM Studio, custom providers
- **Image Generation**: Google Imagen 4.0 (Fast & Premium)
- **AI Function Calling**: 6+ built-in tools (chat history search, summarizer, QR codes, etc.)
- **User Management**: JWT auth, role-based access, BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
- **Admin Dashboard**: Analytics, user management, model configuration
- **Real-time Streaming**: Chat with typing indicators

---

### 📦 Dependencies Highlights
- `@ai-sdk/*` — Vercel AI SDK for multiple providers
- `better-sqlite3` — High-performance SQLite
- `framer-motion` + `gsap` — Animations
- `three` + `@react-three/fiber` — 3D graphics
- `zustand` — State management
- `zod` — Schema validation

---

### ⚡ Available Scripts
```bash
bun dev              # Start dev server + Python sandbox
bun run build        # Production build
bun run setup:db     # Initialize database
bun run admin:create # Create admin user
bun run lint:fix     # Fix ESLint issues
bun run type:check   # TypeScript validation
bun run test         # Run Vitest tests
```

---

### 🔧 Quick Actions
What would you like to do?

1. **Start development** → `bun dev`
2. **Check database** → `bun run db:verify`
3. **Build for production** → `bun run build`
4. **Run tests** → `bun run test`
5. **Review recent changes** → Check `git diff`

Or let me know what specific aspect you'd like to explore!
